BIP-39 mnemonics use 12 or 24 words to deterministically derive wallet keys offline.
Cold (offline) key storage reduces the risk of remote private key theft compared to hot wallets.
Multi-chain wallets rely on chain-specific derivation paths (e.g., BIP44) to produce correct addresses for each chain.
If a seed phrase is compromised, assets on every derived chain are at risk.
Hardware wallets and air-gapped signing keep private keys off internet-connected machines.
